inclusion_criteria: Randomized or quasi-experimental comparative studies (individual or cluster) or diagnostic accuracy studies with a clinical reference standard
Participants are newborns or young infants 0–59 days from low- and middle-income countries
Intervention includes home visits conducted by trained community health workers during the first 59 days of life
Reports at least one outcome: (a) sensitivity/specificity of CHW identification of serious illness, or (b) care-seeking from health facilities
exclusion_criteria: Studies in high-income countries or populations older than 59 days without separable data
Interventions lacking a home-visit component or not delivered by CHWs (e.g., facility-only care, visits solely by nurses/physicians/midwives)
No appropriate comparator (no control group for effectiveness studies or no reference standard for diagnostic accuracy); purely descriptive, qualitative, reviews, or modeling studies
Do not report serious-illness identification performance or care-seeking outcomes
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to identify studies assessing the impact or diagnostic performance of CHW-conducted home visits for newborns and young infants (0–59 days) in LMICs on serious illness identification and care-seeking.

included_articles.title: Increasing access to care for sick newborns: evidence from the Ghana Newhints cluster-randomised controlled trial.
Improvement of perinatal and newborn care in rural Pakistan through community-based strategies: a cluster-randomised effectiveness trial.
